summary: Large Corneria stage decompilation PR focused on replacing many `/// #grCorneria_*` placeholders with C, refining Corneria/stage-related structs and prototypes, and wiring shared smash-taunt/material/item interfaces. decomp.dev reported +3544 matched bytes, +0.09% overall matched code, 7 new exact matches, and 18 additional grcorneria improvements. Review feedback centered on project style and cleanup: prefer `HSD_ASSERT`, reduce raw pointer math, use ABS/MIN/MAX/CLAMP helpers when possible, and scrutinize apparent s32 writes into f32-looking fields.

key_files.role: Main change: +1224/-52 lines, added typed Corneria stage/data structs, implemented or improved many grCorneria routines for stage init, Arwing/background behavior, Great Fox cannon/laser logic, color/material updates, and smash taunt.

Refined ground variable layouts: `grCorneria_GroundVars.xE4` became `Vec3`, several counters/speeds were retyped, `grArwing_GroundVars.xD4/xD8` became s32, and `grSmashTaunt_GroundVars` gained named fields such as `state`, `timer`, `line`, `sis_data_idx`, `sound_id`, `jobj0..2`, `joint_idx0..2`, and `text`.

Promoted several prototypes from `UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS` to typed signatures, including `grCorneria_801DCCFC`, `grCorneria_801DDE88`, `grCorneria_801E03C8`, `grCorneria_801E0678`, `grCorneria_801E08CC`, and `grCorneria_801E277C` taking `grSmashTaunt_GroundVars`.
Made `grMaterial_801C87D0` non-static so Corneria smash-taunt code can reuse the material flag traversal.
Updated Venom to pass `&gp->gv.smashtaunt` into the shared `grCorneria_801E277C` routine instead of `&gp->gv.corneria`.
Typed `it_802EAF34` as `void it_802EAF34(HSD_GObj*, Vec3*, int)`, matching new Great Fox cannon/laser call sites.

what_changed: Replaced numerous grcorneria placeholders with C implementations. decomp.dev listed exact new matches for `grCorneria_801E277C`, `grCorneria_801E0678`, `grCorneria_801DFC98`, `grCorneria_801DFF20`, `grCorneria_801E01A8`, `grCorneria_801DD350`, and `grCorneria_801E2110`.

Improved many still-unmatched Corneria routines, including `grCorneria_801E1BF0`, `grCorneria_801E1348`, `grCorneria_801DE024`, `grCorneria_801DE568`, `grCorneria_801DE8E4`, `grCorneria_801DF8D0`, `grCorneria_801E03C8`, `grCorneria_801E1970`, `grCorneria_801E1060`, `grCorneria_801E2228`, `grCorneria_801DDAC4`, `grCorneria_801E08CC`, `grCorneria_801DDE88`, `grCorneria_801E25C4`, and `grCorneria_801DCCFC`.

Introduced local typed data models in `grcorneria.c`: `grCn_StageData`, `grCn_Data`, and `grCn_Entry`, replacing a raw `void* grCn_804D69A0` with `grCn_StageData*` and naming table areas such as `arwing_gobj`, `arwing_type`, `arwing_group`, `cannon_x`, `cannon_y`, `effect_pos`, `anim_ids`, `positions`, `x3D8`, `x444`, `x458`, `dialog_joints`, and `entries`.
Added helper inlines for common Corneria behavior: `grCn_RandRange`, `grCn_CheckFar`, `grCn_SpawnArwing`, and `grCn_PickUniqueType`.

Implemented Corneria setup and stage state work such as `grCorneria_801DCCFC` and `grCorneria_801DD350`, including stage ID checks via `Stage_80225194`, initializing Arwing slot arrays, spawning ground objects, setting stage_info flags, light setup, and disabling mpLib collision lines.

Implemented or refined Arwing-related updates including object slot registration, animation triggering, visibility based on angle/rotation, audio cues through `lbAudioAx_800237A8`, and cleanup through `Ground_801C4A08`.
Implemented background/segment movement and camera-area selection logic such as `grCorneria_801E01A8`, `grCorneria_801E03C8`, `grCorneria_801E0678`, `grCorneria_801E08CC`, and `grCorneria_801E1060`.
Implemented Great Fox cannon/laser state behavior in `grCorneria_801E1348`, using fighter positions, material animation helpers, effects/sounds, and the newly typed `it_802EAF34` item function.

Moved smash-taunt handling toward a dedicated `grSmashTaunt_GroundVars` layout. `smashTaunt_801E24AC` now uses `gv.smashtaunt.text` as an `HSD_Text*` and toggles `text->hidden`; `grCorneria_801E277C` now takes `grSmashTaunt_GroundVars*` and uses named fields.
smart_moves: Typing `grCn_804D69A0` as `grCn_StageData*` converted many raw offset reads into field accesses like `x38`, `x70`, and `x88`, making stage constants reusable while preserving offset-based names where semantics were still uncertain.
Separating `grSmashTaunt_GroundVars` from generic Corneria vars improved cross-stage correctness: both Corneria and Venom now call `grCorneria_801E277C` with `gv.smashtaunt`.
Exposing `grMaterial_801C87D0` through `grmaterial.h` avoided duplicating material flag traversal logic in `grcorneria.c`.
Using named table fields such as `dialog_joints`, `anim_ids`, `positions`, and `arwing_type` made the remaining address-named code much more searchable than raw `M2C_FIELD` or byte-offset access.
The helper inlines isolate repeated matching-sensitive patterns for random integer ranges, far-camera checks, Arwing spawning, and unique type picking.
The PR kept exact address-based function names for unknown/partially understood routines while still improving signatures and local types, which is useful for incremental decomp work.
decomp_lessons: For large stage files, building partial data structs from observed offsets can unlock many functions without needing final semantic field names.

When the same routine is shared across stages, prefer a specific common sub-struct signature over passing a stage-specific ground-var struct; this PR moved `grCorneria_801E277C` to `grSmashTaunt_GroundVars*` and updated Venom accordingly.
Reviewers prefer `HSD_ASSERT` over direct `__assert` where possible.
Raw `u8*` pointer math and `*(type*)(base + offset)` writes may be expedient for matching, but reviewers will ask whether they can be replaced with typed structs.
Use project macros like `ABS`, `MIN`, `MAX`, and `CLAMP` for obvious absolute-value and clamp patterns when they do not hurt matching.
Be cautious when assigning through `s32*` casts into fields that are or appear to be f32s; if the cast is match-required, leave evidence or improve the field typing when possible.
Small header changes matter: typing an item function or exposing a material helper can remove `UNK_PARAMS` and make stage logic compile cleanly without local fake declarations.
naming_conventions: Corneria implementation names kept the existing address style: `grCorneria_801DCCFC`, `grCorneria_801E277C`, etc.
Local Corneria helpers used the short stage prefix plus descriptive verbs: `grCn_RandRange`, `grCn_CheckFar`, `grCn_SpawnArwing`, `grCn_PickUniqueType`.
New local data structs used the `grCn_` prefix: `grCn_StageData`, `grCn_Data`, and `grCn_Entry`.

Fields with unknown semantics remained offset-style (`xC4`, `x108`, `x3D8`, `x444`, `x458`) while clearer table roles were named (`arwing_gobj`, `arwing_type`, `arwing_group`, `positions`, `dialog_joints`).

The smash-taunt struct was given semantic field names at known offsets: `state`, `timer`, `line`, `sis_data_idx`, `sound_id`, `jobj0`, `jobj1`, `jobj2`, `joint_idx0`, `joint_idx1`, `joint_idx2`, and `text`.
Bitfields stayed conservative as `flags.b0`, `flags.b1`, `flags.b2` when behavior was not fully named.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used `PAD_STACK` in several functions to preserve stack layout while adding C implementations.
Used scoped temporaries and repeated expressions such as `Ground_801C0498()` scale calculations, likely to preserve register/float evaluation order.
Retained some match-oriented casts such as `*(s16*)(v + offset)`, `*(HSD_JObj**)(v + offset)`, and `*(s32*)&field`; review noted these should be cleaned up if a struct layout can be proven.
Converted some M2C-style raw field accesses to typed struct fields, e.g. `grCn_804D69A0->x38` and `((HSD_Text*) gp->gv.smashtaunt.text)->hidden`.

Used exact library calls seen in stage behavior paths: `HSD_JObjSetTranslate*`, `HSD_JObjSetFlagsAll`, `grAnime_801C8098`, `grMaterial_801C8E08/801C8E28`, `Item_80268E5C`, `it_802EAF34`, `mpLib_80057BC0`, and SIS text functions.
Matched progress came from both exact matches and near matches; decomp.dev reported 7 new exact matches and 18 improved unmatched items rather than all new code being fully matched.

review_feedback.feedback: On `grCorneria_801E08CC`, reviewer said direct `__assert("grcorneria.c", 0x9AC, "0")` should probably use `HSD_ASSERT`.
On `grCorneria_801E25C4`, reviewer asked whether the raw pointer math over `void* gv` / `u8* v` could be fixed.
On manual absolute-value code in `grCorneria_801DE568`, reviewer suggested `ABS`, and noted similar availability of `MIN`, `MAX`, and `CLAMP`.
Reviewer questioned why several f32-looking fields were being assigned through s32 writes.
Review history shows an initial CHANGES_REQUESTED review followed shortly by APPROVED; the dump does not include explanatory review body text.

follow_up_queries: grCn_StageData x38 x70 x88 Corneria stage data
grSmashTaunt_GroundVars text HSD_Text hidden Corneria Venom
it_802EAF34 HSD_GObj Vec3 int Great Fox laser
